comments.body: I'm using the latest version 1.11.0. I hadn't looked well. Thank you very much for the link. I will study the examples and build the architecture of my application for the separate schema. Thank you again for the info.

answers.body:
What version of Quarkus are you using? It seems like the latest Quarkus Hibernate ORM version has exactly what you're asking for:

Quarkus currently supports the separate database and the separate schema approach.

From: https://quarkus.io/guides/hibernate-orm#multitenancy

Quarkus does support multitenancy at the Hibernate ORM level (separate schema / separate database), but that only solves data isolation.
In most SaaS applications you also need application-level tenant resolution, for example:

  • extracting the tenant from an HTTP header, JWT claim, request path, or subdomain

  • storing the tenant in a request-scoped context

  • making it accessible in business logic, services, and repositories

Quarkus does not provide this part out of the box, so developers usually implement filters/interceptors + a tenant context manually.

To avoid rewriting this logic in every project, I created a small helper library that provides:

  • TenantContext to access the current tenant anywhere in the code

  • TenantResolver strategies (header, JWT, etc.)

  • configuration properties to enable/disable multitenancy and choose the strategy

Example usage:

quarkus.multi-tenant.enabled=true
quarkus.multi-tenant.strategy=header
quarkus.multi-tenant.header-name=X-Tenant-Id

and then in your application code:

@Inject
TenantContext tenantContext;
String tenantId = tenantContext.getTenantId();

You can find the project here, including examples and documentation:

https://github.com/mathias82/quarkus-multitenancy-helper

It can be useful if you want full multitenancy behavior (tenant extraction + context + Hibernate isolation) without building everything from scratch.

title: Quarkus Multi-tenant Application
body:
I would like to develop a SaaS application (multiple tenants) with Quarkus , but I am facing a problem because Quarkus does not support multi-tenants yet. What to do ? What alternative ? Do you have any idea how I can do this ? Do I have to write everything myself ?

I would like to use Postgresql as the DBMS with the architecture of a unique database and a separate schema for each tenant.
